signals.reasoning: This live trade is decisively broken. BLUR has gone from a peak gain of about $1.21 to a current loss near $-2.34, retaining none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. With a live geopolitical veto active, there is no defensible case for staying in it.
BTC is stable and liquid, but the board is still no-trade and the live veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH has slightly constructive price action, but funding is positive and the board remains no-trade. There is not enough edge here to matter under a veto regime.
SOL is modestly constructive, but positive funding and a no-trade board keep it below threshold. No reason to force it.

ZEC is one of the strongest watchlist names with powerful momentum and negative funding, but it is already extended and the active veto is exactly when strong-looking continuation trades should be downgraded.
DASH has strong momentum and volume, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not actionable right now.
ZEN has a decent profile with improving momentum and only modest funding pressure, but veto conditions still keep it in watch-only territory.
MERL is moving, but the market is thin and the board is still neutral. Not a priority name.
PEOPLE is too thin and too mixed to justify capital. No strong edge on either side.
MAV is the cleaner bearish name on the sheet: negative 24h trend, positive funding, and a distinctly bearish mechanical lean. It is still thin, so conviction stays moderate.
TNSR still has a valid squeeze profile mechanically, but the live geopolitical veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta continuation exposure. Strong watchlist long, not a live trade.
